channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - remapradio.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for remapradio.com

item.title: A Double Remap Newsletter
item.content:encoded:
Remap is taking the week off, but as if often the case as you get older, "days off" are merely being used to plug other holes. I spent the entirety of Monday power washing the backyard after a long delay, mowing the lawn, putting up shelves, and setting up oil change and service appointments for our cars. At this very moment, I am waiting for an oil change to be finished on our minivan, and was quickly informed there is also a mandatory safety recall to be done, as well. Tremendous. And my rewards for all of this? One of the gnarliest Chicago heat waves in decades. My heart, however, remains full, after several days of playing very fun video games with very good people and the very good audience of Remap. It balances out.

-pk, who is taking a proper vacation later in July

  • ARTICLE: "Zoroark and the Invention of Lying" by Bruno Dias – Plumbing the depths of Pokémon Champions, and the deceitful mind of a competitive player.
  • PODCAST: Sports! (feat: Rob, Patrick) – Has it really been [checks notes] four months since an episode of Sports!? Don’t worry, your patience will be rewarded, especially if you wait until the end, when Patrick reveals a story he’s been sitting on for weeks that had Rob trying to avoid breaking his monitor. In more pleasant moments, Rob and Patrick weigh their options if a genie granted them tickets guaranteed to make their team lose, the NBA playoffs, and, of course, an early Bears season prediction.
  • STREAM: Dark Scrolls, Sadly, Doesn't Have the Juice (feat: Patrick, Janet, Chia) – Janet and Patrick dip into the new action platformer Dark Scrolls, from the developers of Gato Roboto and Gunbrella, before exploring frogs and vaulting.
  • PODCAST: Remap Radio (feat: Janet, Patrick, Rob, Chia) – Sit down and prepare for a tale of woe, misery, and humiliation in the world of Marathon, before Rob, Patrick, Chia, and Janet go down a gunpla rabbit hole, try to figure out who the newly expensive Steam Machine is for, and share thoughts a number of games, including Silver Pines, Tethergeist, Star Fox, About Fishing, Iron Nest, and more. We also talk through an AI jump scare.
  • PODCAST: Rob's First Costco (feat: Rob, Chia, Patrick) – Patrick, Mr. Kirkland himself, has made the trip to the east coast to meet up with Chia and Rob, which can only mean one thing: Rob's breaking in his brand new Costco membership under the tutelage of the master himself. Listen in as we wander the liminal space of the Costco parking lot, marvel at the sights and sounds of the Costco produce fridge, and pay our respects to the humble chicken bake.
  • STREAM: Live From Highground [June 2026] — Day One! – Patrick, Rob, and Chia are together for a weekend of streaming from Highground!
  • STREAM: Live From Highground [June 2026] — Day Two! – You know, at this point, we might as well just realize we're going to play golf all weekend.
  • PODCAST: HOA (feat: Rob, Patrick) – The HOA returns to mull and consider life’s many challenges, as Patrick and Rob unpack what happens when your movers start verbally fighting, what to do when you walk into a house that makes you look at your current one in shame, and…well, Rob’s lawn was kind of on fire? Plus, how many times can you call Intex back to fight with them over a warranty they keep rejecting?

If you have any support issues, send details to support@remapradio.com

Patrick Klepek (he/him) is an editor at Remap. In another life, he worked on horror movie sets, but instead, he also runs Crossplay, a newsletter about parenting and video games. You can follow him on Twitter, Threads, Mastodon, and Bluesky.
